data.narrative:
Wyvern is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Chris Robson, Callie Lissinna, Kurtis Broda, and Kristen Cote, with $16M raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage peers in the same sector, including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $32M. Peer-Median anchors to the median valuation of 4 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comps—ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), Sift ($274M Series B), and Aalyria ($1.3B Series B)—producing a $400M implied valuation. The range across these peers spans $274M to $1.3B. Public-Comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 59.8x across the 5 tickers) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue.

The triangulated range is $27M to $460M, with a median of $216M across the two methods. Stage-Progression yields the lower bound, while Peer-Median drives the upper anchor. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in private peer valuations (4.7x range), the stage heterogeneity within the comp set, and the absence of revenue data to validate public multiples.
